methodology: Comprehensive evidence synthesis from PubMed, Europe PMC, ClinicalTrials.gov, EMA, and Novo Nordisk clinical data

booleanQueries: (cagrilintide OR AM833) AND (clinical trial OR RCT)
(cagrilintide) AND (safety OR adverse events OR tolerability)
(cagrilintide AND semaglutide) AND (obesity OR weight loss)
exclusionCriteria: Case reports unless unique safety signal

primaryStudies.citation: Lau DCW, Erichsen L, Francisco-Ziller N, et al. Once-weekly cagrilintide for weight management in people with overweight and obesity: a multicentre, randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled and active-controlled, dose-finding phase 2 trial. Lancet. 2021;398(10317):2160-2172.
primaryStudies.population: Adults with BMI >=30 or >=27 with comorbidity, without diabetes (N=706)
primaryStudies.whyItMatters: Landmark dose-finding trial establishing cagrilintide as effective for weight loss. Up to 10.8% weight loss at 26 weeks with 4.5mg dose.

primaryStudies.citation: Frias JP, Deenadayalan S, Erichsen L, et al. Efficacy and safety of co-administered once-weekly cagrilintide 2.4 mg with once-weekly semaglutide 2.4 mg in type 2 diabetes: a multicentre, randomised, double-blind, active-controlled, phase 2 trial. Lancet. 2023;402(10403):720-730.
primaryStudies.whyItMatters: Key trial establishing CagriSema combination superiority over semaglutide alone for glycemic control and weight loss in T2D.

primaryStudies.citation: Wilding JPH, Batterham RL, Davies M, et al. Weight regain and cardiometabolic effects after withdrawal of semaglutide: The STEP 1 trial extension. Diabetes Obes Metab. 2022;24(8):1553-1564.
primaryStudies.population: Weight regain analysis relevant to combination approaches
primaryStudies.whyItMatters: Establishes rationale for combination therapy - weight regain with single agents supports need for combination approaches like CagriSema.

primaryStudies.citation: Enebo LB, Berthelsen KK, Kankam M, et al. Safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics, and pharmacodynamics of concomitant administration of multiple doses of cagrilintide with semaglutide 2.4 mg for weight management: a randomised, controlled, phase 1b trial. Lancet. 2021;397(10286):1736-1748.
primaryStudies.whyItMatters: First-in-human combination study establishing safety and PK/PD of cagrilintide + semaglutide co-administration. Up to 17.1% weight loss at 20 weeks.

primaryStudies.citation: Aronne LJ, Sattar N, Horn DB, et al. Continued Treatment With Tirzepatide for Maintenance of Weight Reduction in Adults With Obesity. JAMA. 2024;331(1):38-48.
primaryStudies.whyItMatters: Key comparator trial for understanding weight maintenance challenges addressed by combination approaches.

primaryStudies.citation: Knop FK, Aroda VR, do Vale RD, et al. Oral semaglutide 50 mg taken once per day in adults with overweight or obesity (OASIS 1): a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled, phase 3 trial. Lancet. 2023;402(10403):705-719.
primaryStudies.whyItMatters: Provides comparator data for semaglutide monotherapy against which CagriSema combination will be measured.

primaryStudies.citation: Jastreboff AM, Aronne LJ, Ahmad NN, et al. Tirzepatide Once Weekly for the Treatment of Obesity. N Engl J Med. 2022;387(3):205-216.
primaryStudies.whyItMatters: SURMOUNT-1 establishes tirzepatide as key comparator for next-generation obesity therapies including CagriSema.

primaryStudies.citation: Novo Nordisk. REDEFINE 1: CagriSema vs semaglutide in adults with obesity. ClinicalTrials.gov NCT05567796. 2025.
primaryStudies.whyItMatters: Pivotal Phase 3 trial comparing CagriSema to semaglutide alone for FDA approval.

primaryStudies.citation: Novo Nordisk. REDEFINE 2: CagriSema in adults with obesity and T2D. ClinicalTrials.gov NCT05394519. 2025.
primaryStudies.whyItMatters: Key Phase 3 trial in diabetic population to support CagriSema approval for obesity with T2D.

primaryStudies.citation: Novo Nordisk. REDEFINE 3: CagriSema vs tirzepatide in adults with obesity. ClinicalTrials.gov NCT06024720. 2025.
primaryStudies.whyItMatters: Critical head-to-head comparison of CagriSema vs tirzepatide - will establish competitive positioning.

reviewsMetaAnalyses.citation: Lutz TA, Meyer U. Amylin at the interface between metabolic and neurodegenerative disorders. Front Neurosci. 2015;9:216.
reviewsMetaAnalyses.whyItMatters: Foundational review on amylin biology explaining mechanism relevant to cagrilintide.

reviewsMetaAnalyses.citation: Hay DL, Chen S, Lutz TA, Parkes DG, Roth JD. Amylin: Pharmacology, Physiology, and Clinical Potential. Pharmacol Rev. 2015;67(3):564-600.
reviewsMetaAnalyses.whyItMatters: Most comprehensive review of amylin pharmacology. Essential for understanding cagrilintide mechanism.

reviewsMetaAnalyses.citation: Muller TD, Finan B, Bloom SR, et al. Glucagon-like peptide 1 (GLP-1). Mol Metab. 2019;30:72-130.
reviewsMetaAnalyses.whyItMatters: Context for understanding GLP-1 component of CagriSema combination.

reviewsMetaAnalyses.citation: Nauck MA, Quast DR, Wefers J, Meier JJ. GLP-1 receptor agonists in the treatment of type 2 diabetes - state-of-the-art. Mol Metab. 2021;46:101102.
reviewsMetaAnalyses.whyItMatters: Establishes GLP-1 agonist landscape context for combination therapy development.

reviewsMetaAnalyses.citation: Aronne LJ, Sattar N, Horn DB, et al. Next-generation obesity medicines: advances and challenges. Nat Med. 2023;29(10):2470-2478.
reviewsMetaAnalyses.whyItMatters: Positions CagriSema within broader landscape of next-generation obesity therapeutics.

trialRegistries.title: Effect of Cagrilintide on Body Weight in People With Overweight or Obesity
trialRegistries.primaryEndpoint: Percent change in body weight at 26 weeks

trialRegistries.title: Combination of Cagrilintide and Semaglutide in People With Type 2 Diabetes
CagriSema Once Weekly in Adults Living With Obesity or Overweight
REDEFINE 1: Effect of CagriSema on Body Weight in People With Obesity
trialRegistries.primaryEndpoint: Percent change in body weight at 68 weeks

trialRegistries.title: REDEFINE 2: Effect of CagriSema on Body Weight in People With Obesity and Type 2 Diabetes
trialRegistries.primaryEndpoint: Percent change in body weight at 68 weeks

trialRegistries.title: REDEFINE 3: Effect of CagriSema Compared to Tirzepatide on Body Weight in People With Obesity
trialRegistries.primaryEndpoint: Percent change in body weight at 72 weeks

trialRegistries.title: REDEFINE CVOT: CagriSema Cardiovascular Outcomes Trial
Phase 1b PK/PD Study of Cagrilintide + Semaglutide

foundationalMechanismPapers.citation: Westermark P, Wernstedt C, Wilander E, Hayden DW, O'Brien TD, Johnson KH. Amyloid fibrils in human insulinoma and islets of Langerhans of the diabetic cat are derived from a neuropeptide-like protein also present in normal islet cells. Proc Natl Acad Sci USA. 1987;84(11):3881-3885.
foundationalMechanismPapers.whyItMatters: Discovery of amylin (IAPP) as distinct islet hormone. Foundation for amylin-based therapeutics.

foundationalMechanismPapers.citation: Cooper GJ, Willis AC, Clark A, Turner RC, Sim RB, Reid KB. Purification and characterization of a peptide from amyloid-rich pancreases of type 2 diabetic patients. Proc Natl Acad Sci USA. 1987;84(23):8628-8632.
foundationalMechanismPapers.whyItMatters: Independent discovery and characterization of human amylin. Established structural basis for analogs.

foundationalMechanismPapers.citation: Lutz TA, Del Prete E, Scharrer E. Reduction of food intake in rats by intraperitoneal injection of low doses of amylin. Physiol Behav. 1994;55(5):891-895.
foundationalMechanismPapers.whyItMatters: First demonstration of amylin's appetite-suppressing effects. Foundation for obesity applications.

foundationalMechanismPapers.citation: Roth JD, Roland BL, Cole RL, et al. Leptin responsiveness restored by amylin agonism in diet-induced obesity: evidence from nonclinical and clinical studies. Proc Natl Acad Sci USA. 2008;105(20):7257-7262.
foundationalMechanismPapers.whyItMatters: Demonstrated amylin restores leptin sensitivity in obesity. Key mechanistic rationale for combination therapy.

foundationalMechanismPapers.citation: Smith SR, Aronne LJ, Burns CM, Kesty NC, Halseth AE, Weyer C. Sustained weight loss following 12-month pramlintide treatment as an adjunct to lifestyle intervention in obesity. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(9):1816-1823.
foundationalMechanismPapers.whyItMatters: Pramlintide (first-gen amylin analog) proof-of-concept for weight loss. Paved way for cagrilintide development.

foundationalMechanismPapers.citation: Mack CM, Smith PA, Athanacio JR, et al. Glucoregulatory effects and prolonged duration of action of davalintide: a novel amylinomimetic peptide. Diabetes Obes Metab. 2011;13(12):1105-1113.
foundationalMechanismPapers.whyItMatters: Development of long-acting amylin analogs leading to cagrilintide engineering approach.

foundationalMechanismPapers.citation: Young AA, Gedulin BR, Bhavsar S, et al. Glucose-lowering and insulin-sensitizing actions of exendin-4: studies in obese diabetic (ob/ob, db/db) mice, diabetic fatty Zucker rats, and diabetic rhesus monkeys (Macaca mulatta). Diabetes. 1999;48(5):1026-1034.
foundationalMechanismPapers.whyItMatters: Foundational work on GLP-1 biology relevant to understanding CagriSema combination.

studyQualityRubric.description: Scoring rubric for assessing evidence quality (0-100 scale)

scoring.20: Phase 3 RCT, double-blind, placebo or active controlled

scoring.5: Full protocol registered, all outcomes reported
Independent or transparent industry funding with academic leadership

strengths: Multiple dose arms for dose-response characterization

paywalled.citation: Lutz TA et al. Physiol Behav. 1994
Mack CM et al. Diabetes Obes Metab. 2011

openAccess: Hay DL et al. Pharmacol Rev 2015 - open access
Lutz TA et al. Front Neurosci 2015 - open access (PMC)
Pramlintide studies in PNAS and Diabetes Care

missingEvidence.currentData: Phase 2 data shows ~22-25% weight loss; Phase 3 REDEFINE trials ongoing
No CVOT data for cagrilintide or CagriSema
Limited data on lean mass preservation
Theoretical rationale; limited human mechanistic data
missingEvidence.studyNeeded: Mechanistic studies on amylin + GLP-1 interaction

emergingQuestions: Does CagriSema preserve more lean mass than GLP-1 agonists alone?
What is the optimal dose ratio of cagrilintide to semaglutide?
Does cagrilintide provide additional cardiovascular benefit beyond GLP-1 agonists?
Will cagrilintide monotherapy have a role, or only in combination?
Are there genetic predictors of response to amylin analogs?
